refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable execution of the load→travel→dump cycle within the customer-defined cycle time target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR conflates two distinct obligations: (1) supporting/enabling the capability to achieve cycle time, and (2) actually achieving the measured KPI. The refined FR is atomic and solution-neutral by focusing on the functional obligation—enabling the cycle to execute within the time envelope—without prescribing how (e.g., via speed optimization, path planning, operator training, or equipment design). The actual achievement of p95 ≤ CT_target is a verification criterion, not a functional requirement. This separation allows DPs to address enablement independently of operational performance measurement.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Demonstrate that under nominal duty cycle and site conditions, the NGHE solution's design permits completion of load→travel→dump without artificial time constraints. Measure p95 cycle time in acceptance testing; if p95 > CT_target, root-cause analysis determines whether the failure is in FR realization (DP/IM inadequacy) or external factors (operator skill, site conditions, maintenance state).
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-002.1: The NGHE solution shall enable load acquisition within the cycle time budget.
FR-NGHE-002.2: The NGHE solution shall enable travel (loaded and unloaded) within the cycle time budget.
FR-NGHE-002.3: The NGHE solution shall enable material dump within the cycle time budget.
FR-NGHE-002.4: The NGHE solution shall minimize idle/transition time between load, travel, and dump phases.

candidate_dps: DP1: Decompose cycle into discrete, sequenced phases (load, travel, dump) with phase-gating logic that enforces strict ordering and allows independent optimization of each phase duration.

DP2: Implement a centralized cycle-time controller that monitors elapsed time against phase budgets and dynamically adjusts operational parameters (speed, acceleration, dwell) to meet p95 target without exceeding safety margins.

DP3: Design modular subsystem interfaces (load mechanism, propulsion, dump mechanism) such that each subsystem exposes cycle-phase-relevant performance metrics (load time, travel time, dump time) enabling real-time cycle accounting and bottleneck visibility.

DP4: Establish a duty-cycle characterization model (payload distribution, terrain profile, ambient conditions) and use it to pre-compute safe operating envelopes for each phase, with runtime validation against measured site conditions.
DP5: Implement redundant or fail-safe timing and sequencing logic to ensure that cycle-time measurement and phase transitions remain valid even under partial subsystem degradation or sensor drift.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1 + DP3 + DP4 (integrated approach): Decompose the load→travel→dump cycle into independently optimizable phases with strict phase-gating; design subsystem interfaces to expose phase-relevant performance metrics; establish duty-cycle characterization and safe operating envelopes validated at runtime against site conditions. This combination decouples phase optimization from subsystem implementation details, enables transparent cycle accounting, and grounds performance targets in measurable site conditions.

potential_sources: DP1 ↔ DP3: Phase-gating logic must align with subsystem interface contracts (e.g., load-phase completion signal must be unambiguous and subsystem-agnostic). Risk: tight coupling if phase gates are hardcoded to specific subsystem implementations.

DP3 ↔ DP4: Subsystem metrics (e.g., load time) must be comparable across duty-cycle scenarios. Risk: if metrics are not normalized for payload or terrain, coupling between subsystem design and duty-cycle model increases.

DP4 ↔ Runtime: Safe operating envelopes must be validated against real site conditions without requiring redesign. Risk: if envelopes are too conservative or too aggressive, runtime validation may fail or cycle time may exceed target.

Implicit coupling: Cycle-time target (CT_target) is a constraint, not a DP output. If CT_target is unrealistic for the duty cycle, no DP can satisfy it. Recommend explicit feasibility check before design proceeds.
coupling_check.notes: Coupling is manageable if subsystem interfaces are defined as abstract phase-completion signals (not implementation-specific) and duty-cycle characterization is treated as a living model updated with site data. Avoid tight coupling by ensuring phase-gating logic does not depend on subsystem internals.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: CT_target is feasible given the duty cycle, payload range, and site conditions. RISK: If target is derived from legacy equipment or optimistic estimates, it may be unachievable. MITIGATION: Conduct feasibility study (DP4 duty-cycle modeling) before committing to target.

ASSUMPTION: Subsystem performance (load time, travel time, dump time) is repeatable and measurable under site conditions. RISK: High variability in payload, terrain, or operator behavior may make p95 target difficult to meet consistently. MITIGATION: Implement real-time cycle monitoring and feedback to operators; design for robustness to variability.

ASSUMPTION: Phase-gating logic can be implemented independently of subsystem control. RISK: If subsystems are tightly coupled (e.g., load and travel phases overlap), phase-gating becomes complex and error-prone. MITIGATION: Validate phase independence during subsystem design (DP3).

ASSUMPTION: Safety plan and operational policies do not impose hard constraints that conflict with cycle-time optimization. RISK: Safety dwell times, speed limits, or operator approval gates may consume significant cycle time. MITIGATION: Integrate safety constraints into duty-cycle model (DP4) early.

ASSUMPTION: Measurement of p95 cycle time is accurate and representative. RISK: Measurement errors, outliers, or non-representative duty cycles may invalidate acceptance criteria. MITIGATION: Define measurement protocol and duty-cycle sampling strategy as part of acceptance criteria.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine FR to explicitly state: (1) the duty-cycle characterization (payload range, terrain profile, ambient conditions, operator behavior model); (2) the definition of cycle-time measurement (start/end events, outlier handling, sample size for p95); (3) the feasibility constraint (is CT_target derived from customer requirement, legacy baseline, or engineering estimate?). Introduce a secondary FR: 'The NGHE solution shall provide real-time visibility into cycle-phase durations and subsystem performance metrics to enable continuous monitoring and operator feedback.' This supports DP3 and DP4 and reduces risk of undetected drift or variability. Consider adding a tertiary FR for robustness: 'The NGHE solution shall maintain cycle-time performance within acceptable bounds under ±10% variations in payload, terrain, and ambient conditions.' This grounds DP4 in explicit robustness targets.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Integrated cycle orchestration, phase-gating logic, duty-cycle characterization, safe operating envelope validation, and subsystem interface abstraction for load→travel→dump decomposition

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Inbound: site conditions, subsystem performance metrics, phase-state feedback; Outbound: phase-gating commands, duty-cycle accounting, safe operating envelope status

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Mission objectives and cycle parameters; NGHE Solution translates into phase-sequenced task directives

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Runtime validation failures, envelope violations, phase-gating holds, and safe-state transitions

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Distributed phase coordination and subsystem state synchronization across Vehicle and Teleoperation domains

module_containment_scores.notes: Highest cohesion: cycle decomposition, phase-gating, duty-cycle characterization, and envelope validation are all orchestration-layer concerns. DP1+DP3+DP4 define the control logic and metrics abstraction that NGHE Solution naturally owns. Minor diffusion: subsystem implementations (Vehicle) must conform to exposed interfaces, but logic remains centralized.

Poor fit: Vehicle is a physical/execution layer. Phase-gating, duty-cycle accounting, and safe operating envelope validation are orchestration concerns that would scatter across load, travel, and dump subsystems. Tight coupling to site-condition monitoring and cross-phase coordination logic would diffuse responsibility.

Weak fit: Teleoperation is a human-interface and remote-control layer. Cycle decomposition and phase-gating are autonomous orchestration logic, not operator-facing. Envelope validation and duty-cycle characterization belong in autonomous domain, not teleoperation.

Partial fit: Safe operating envelope validation and runtime safety checks align with Safety Services scope. However, phase-gating logic, duty-cycle characterization, and cycle decomposition are not safety-specific; they are performance-optimization and orchestration concerns. Splitting DP across Safety Services and NGHE Solution creates coupling and diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: DP1 + DP3 + DP4 form a cohesive orchestration strategy that decouples phase optimization from subsystem implementation. This is fundamentally an autonomous mission-planning and cycle-management concern, not a vehicle subsystem, teleoperation interface, or safety-only function. NGHE Solution (Next-Generation Hauling Engine or equivalent mission orchestration layer) is the natural owner because it sits at the abstraction level where cycle decomposition, phase-gating, and duty-cycle accounting are defined and enforced. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services consume the phase-gating directives and envelope constraints, but do not own the logic.
containment_risks: Risk: If NGHE Solution is not yet established as a distinct architectural layer, DP logic may diffuse into Vehicle subsystems, creating tight coupling between phase-gating and load/travel/dump implementations.

Risk: Duty-cycle characterization requires real-time site-condition feedback (Telemetry). If Telemetry interface is not well-defined, NGHE Solution may become tightly coupled to specific sensor implementations.
Risk: Safe operating envelope validation overlaps with Safety Services scope. Without clear interface contracts (SafetyAlert), envelope logic may duplicate or conflict with safety checks.
Risk: Phase-gating commands must be enforced by Vehicle subsystems. If Vehicle does not respect phase boundaries, NGHE Solution loses control authority and DP becomes ineffective.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: No revision to DP choice required. DP1 + DP3 + DP4 is well-contained in NGHE Solution. However, strengthen the DP specification by explicitly defining: (1) phase-gating interface contract (which commands Vehicle must honor), (2) Telemetry schema for site-condition inputs and phase-state feedback, (3) SafetyAlert escalation rules for envelope violations, and (4) duty-cycle accounting metrics exposed via Telemetry. This ensures NGHE Solution remains the single source of truth for cycle orchestration while Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services operate as disciplined consumers of its directives.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP1 + DP3 + DP4 directly address the FR requirement to enable execution of load→travel→dump cycle within customer-defined cycle time target. DP1 (phase decomposition with strict gating) enables independent optimization of each phase; DP3 (subsystem interface abstraction) exposes performance metrics needed for cycle accounting; DP4 (duty-cycle characterization and runtime envelope validation) grounds targets in measurable site conditions. The combination is logically sound and sufficient to satisfy the FR. No logical gaps detected.

diffusion_risks: CRITICAL: Phase-gating logic enforcement depends on Vehicle subsystem compliance. If Vehicle load/travel/dump implementations do not respect phase-boundary commands from NGHE Solution, DP1 loses control authority. Recommend explicit phase-gating interface contract (e.g., state machine, command acknowledgment protocol) to prevent diffusion of gating logic into Vehicle.

HIGH: Duty-cycle characterization (DP3) requires real-time site-condition telemetry (soil type, slope, load mass, ambient conditions). If Telemetry schema is not formally defined, NGHE Solution risks tight coupling to specific sensor implementations or ad-hoc data formats. Recommend schema specification in DP3 refinement.

HIGH: Safe operating envelope validation (DP4) overlaps with Safety Services scope. Risk of logic duplication or conflicting decisions if SafetyAlert interface is not clearly defined. Recommend explicit escalation rules: which envelope violations trigger SafetyAlert vs. phase-gating holds vs. operator warnings.

MEDIUM: Duty-cycle accounting metrics (DP3) must be exposed via Telemetry outbound interface. If metrics are not standardized (e.g., phase duration, subsystem utilization, envelope margin), downstream consumers (Teleoperation, Safety Services) cannot reliably interpret cycle performance. Recommend metric definitions in DP3.

MEDIUM: NGHE Solution containment score of 0.92 is strong, but 0.08 diffusion remains. This is primarily due to Vehicle subsystems needing to conform to exposed interfaces. Mitigation: formalize interface contracts (phase-gating commands, metric schemas) to ensure Vehicle acts as a disciplined consumer, not a co-owner of orchestration logic.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the correct architectural owner for DP1 + DP3 + DP4. Cycle decomposition, phase-gating, duty-cycle characterization, and safe operating envelope validation are fundamentally orchestration-layer concerns that sit above Vehicle subsystem implementations. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are consumers of NGHE Solution directives, not co-owners. The 0.92 containment score reflects high cohesion of DP logic within NGHE Solution. Remaining diffusion risk is primarily interface-definition risk (DP<->IM bridge), not logic scatter. Recommend strengthening interface contracts to eliminate this residual risk.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: FEASIBLE with interface clarity. Acceptance criterion 'p95 ≤ CT_target under agreed duty cycle and site conditions' is measurable via Telemetry outbound (phase-gating commands, duty-cycle accounting, envelope status). NGHE Solution must expose: (1) phase-state transitions with timestamps, (2) duty-cycle metrics (phase durations, subsystem utilization), (3) site-condition inputs used for envelope validation. Vehicle must report phase-completion feedback and subsystem performance metrics. Telemetry interface must be formally specified to enable cycle-time measurement and post-hoc analysis. Worksite safety plan compliance is verified via SafetyAlert outbound (envelope violations, phase-gating holds, safe-state transitions).
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: CLEAR. FR is allocated to NGHE Solution as the orchestration layer. Cycle time target is achieved through phase-gating (DP1), subsystem interface abstraction (DP3), and duty-cycle validation (DP4). Allocation is unambiguous: NGHE Solution owns orchestration logic; Vehicle owns load/travel/dump execution; Telemetry owns sensor data and metric reporting; SafetyAlert owns safety escalation. No ownership ambiguity detected. However, recommend explicit allocation statement in design documentation: 'NGHE Solution is responsible for cycle decomposition, phase-gating, and duty-cycle accounting. Vehicle is responsible for phase execution and metric reporting. Telemetry is responsible for site-condition monitoring and metric transport. Safety Services is responsible for envelope violation escalation.' This clarifies boundaries and prevents diffusion.

recommended_revisions: PRIORITY 1 (Interface Contracts): Formalize phase-gating interface specification. Define: (a) phase-state machine (load, travel, dump, idle, hold, safe-stop), (b) command protocol (e.g., phase-transition requests, acknowledgments, timeouts), (c) Vehicle compliance requirements (e.g., 'Vehicle must not initiate travel until load phase is complete and gating command is received'). This prevents diffusion of gating logic into Vehicle.

PRIORITY 1 (Telemetry Schema): Define Telemetry inbound schema for site-condition inputs (soil type, slope angle, load mass, ambient temperature, etc.) and subsystem performance metrics (phase duration, subsystem utilization, power consumption, etc.). Define Telemetry outbound schema for phase-gating commands, duty-cycle accounting (phase times, cycle time, envelope margin), and safe operating envelope status. This ensures NGHE Solution is not tightly coupled to specific sensor implementations.

PRIORITY 1 (SafetyAlert Escalation): Define escalation rules for safe operating envelope violations. Specify: (a) which violations trigger immediate SafetyAlert (e.g., structural load exceeds limit), (b) which violations trigger phase-gating holds (e.g., duty-cycle margin below threshold), (c) which violations trigger operator warnings (e.g., envelope margin < 10%). This prevents logic duplication between NGHE Solution and Safety Services.

PRIORITY 2 (Duty-Cycle Metrics): Expand DP3 to explicitly define duty-cycle characterization metrics: (a) phase-duration targets and tolerances, (b) subsystem utilization profiles (load subsystem, travel subsystem, dump subsystem), (c) envelope margin calculation (e.g., 'margin = (envelope_limit - current_load) / envelope_limit'), (d) site-condition adjustment factors (e.g., 'cycle_time_adjusted = cycle_time_nominal * f(slope, soil_type)'). This grounds DP3 in measurable quantities.

PRIORITY 2 (Vehicle Compliance): Add acceptance criterion for Vehicle: 'Vehicle subsystems must respect phase-gating commands and report phase-completion feedback within [timeout] ms. Phase-gating hold must prevent subsystem state transitions.' This ensures Vehicle acts as a disciplined consumer of NGHE Solution directives.

PRIORITY 3 (Teleoperation Interface): Clarify Teleoperation's role in cycle execution. Recommend: Teleoperation receives phase-state feedback and duty-cycle accounting from NGHE Solution (read-only). Teleoperation can request cycle abort or safe-stop, but cannot override phase-gating logic. This prevents operator interference with autonomous orchestration.

PRIORITY 3 (Network Link Specification): Define Network Link protocol for distributed phase coordination (Vehicle and Teleoperation domains). Specify: (a) message types (phase-gating commands, phase-state feedback, telemetry, safety alerts), (b) latency requirements (e.g., phase-gating command delivery < 100 ms), (c) failure modes (e.g., 'if Network Link fails, Vehicle enters safe-stop state'). This ensures NGHE Solution can reliably orchestrate across distributed domains.

decision.justification: Gate results show FR-DP-IM alignment is logically sound (all gates pass), but critical interface-definition gaps exist at the DP↔IM bridge. The 7 recommended revisions—particularly PRIORITY 1 items (phase-gating interface contract, Telemetry schema, SafetyAlert escalation rules)—are not refinements of existing DP logic but rather new functional requirements that must be decomposed before implementation can proceed. High safety criticality amplifies the risk of proceeding without explicit sub-FR coverage of interface contracts and failure modes. Recommend ZIGZAG_DOWN to derive sub-FRs that address: (1) phase-gating command protocol and Vehicle compliance enforcement, (2) Telemetry schema definition and site-condition monitoring, (3) SafetyAlert escalation logic and envelope violation handling, (4) Network Link reliability and distributed coordination. These sub-FRs will close the DP↔IM diffusion gaps and ground the design in measurable interface specifications.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1.1: Enforce phase-state machine transitions via explicit command protocol. Vehicle must not initiate phase transitions without phase-gating command from NGHE Solution, and must acknowledge phase-completion feedback within defined timeout.

FR1.2: Define and validate phase-gating command interface contract. Specify phase-state machine (load, travel, dump, idle, hold, safe-stop), command acknowledgment protocol, timeout handling, and Vehicle compliance requirements.
FR2.1: Acquire and normalize site-condition telemetry (soil type, slope angle, load mass, ambient conditions) to enable duty-cycle characterization and safe operating envelope validation.

FR2.2: Define Telemetry inbound schema for site-condition inputs and subsystem performance metrics. Ensure NGHE Solution is decoupled from specific sensor implementations via standardized metric definitions.
FR3.1: Detect safe operating envelope violations (structural load, thermal limits, duty-cycle margin) and escalate to Safety Services with severity classification.

FR3.2: Define SafetyAlert escalation rules: specify which envelope violations trigger immediate SafetyAlert (critical), which trigger phase-gating holds (warning), and which trigger operator notifications (advisory). Prevent logic duplication with Safety Services.

FR4.1: Ensure reliable distributed phase coordination across Vehicle and Teleoperation domains via Network Link. Define message types, latency requirements, and failure-mode handling (e.g., safe-stop on link loss).
FR4.2: Define Network Link protocol specification for phase-gating commands, phase-state feedback, telemetry transport, and safety alerts. Specify latency budgets and acknowledgment mechanisms.

revise.why: DP1 (phase decomposition with strict gating), DP3 (subsystem interface abstraction), and DP4 (duty-cycle characterization) are logically correct but under-specified at the interface level. The recommended revisions indicate that DP1 requires explicit phase-gating command protocol (DP1.1), DP3 requires Telemetry schema definition (DP3.1), and DP4 requires SafetyAlert escalation rules (DP4.1). Additionally, a new DP is needed for Network Link protocol specification (DP5). Revise current-level DPs to include interface-contract specifications, or decompose into sub-DPs that address interface definition, schema standardization, and protocol specification. This ensures DP↔IM bridge is explicit and verifiable before implementation.

assumptions: Vehicle subsystems will comply with phase-gating commands if interface contract is formally specified and enforced.
Telemetry system can provide site-condition inputs (soil type, slope, load mass) with sufficient accuracy and latency to enable real-time duty-cycle validation.
SafetyAlert system can receive envelope violation notifications from NGHE Solution and escalate appropriately without duplicating logic.
Network Link between Vehicle, Teleoperation, and NGHE Solution has sufficient bandwidth and reliability to support phase-gating commands and telemetry feedback within latency budgets.
Operator (Teleoperation) will not override phase-gating logic and will respect NGHE Solution orchestration directives.
Site conditions (soil type, slope, load mass) are known or measurable at runtime and can be used to adjust cycle-time targets dynamically.
open_risks: CRITICAL: Phase-gating interface contract not yet formalized. Risk of Vehicle subsystems ignoring phase-gating commands or initiating transitions independently, causing cycle-time violations or safety hazards. Mitigation: Derive FR1.2 and specify command protocol with acknowledgment and timeout mechanisms.

CRITICAL: SafetyAlert escalation rules not yet defined. Risk of logic duplication between NGHE Solution and Safety Services, or conflicting decisions on envelope violations. Mitigation: Derive FR3.2 and define escalation thresholds (critical vs. warning vs. advisory) with clear ownership boundaries.

HIGH: Telemetry schema not yet standardized. Risk of NGHE Solution tight coupling to specific sensor implementations or ad-hoc data formats. Mitigation: Derive FR2.2 and define inbound/outbound schema for site-condition inputs and duty-cycle metrics.

HIGH: Network Link protocol not yet specified. Risk of phase-gating command delivery delays, packet loss, or link failures causing phase-coordination failures. Mitigation: Derive FR4.2 and specify latency budgets, acknowledgment mechanisms, and safe-stop behavior on link loss.

MEDIUM: Duty-cycle characterization metrics not yet formally defined. Risk of inconsistent cycle-time accounting or misalignment between NGHE Solution and downstream consumers (Teleoperation, Safety Services). Mitigation: Expand DP3 to define phase-duration targets, subsystem utilization profiles, envelope margin calculation, and site-condition adjustment factors.

MEDIUM: Vehicle compliance acceptance criteria not yet specified. Risk of Vehicle subsystems not respecting phase-gating holds or reporting metrics inconsistently. Mitigation: Derive FR1.1 and define Vehicle acceptance criteria for phase-gating compliance and feedback reporting.
evidence_needed: Phase-gating command protocol specification (state machine, message format, acknowledgment protocol, timeout values).
Telemetry schema definition (inbound: soil type, slope, load mass, ambient conditions; outbound: phase-gating commands, phase-state feedback, duty-cycle metrics, envelope status).
SafetyAlert escalation rules (critical thresholds triggering immediate alert, warning thresholds triggering phase-gating holds, advisory thresholds triggering operator notifications).
Network Link protocol specification (message types, latency requirements, failure-mode handling, safe-stop behavior on link loss).
Vehicle compliance acceptance criteria (phase-gating command acknowledgment timeout, phase-completion feedback format, subsystem metric reporting schema).
Duty-cycle characterization metrics definition (phase-duration targets, subsystem utilization profiles, envelope margin calculation, site-condition adjustment factors).
Teleoperation interface specification (read-only access to phase-state and duty-cycle metrics, cycle abort/safe-stop request protocol, override restrictions).
Site-condition measurement and normalization procedure (soil type classification, slope angle measurement, load mass estimation, ambient condition sensing).
